headline: Agent reliability moves to the front as new releases stress tool use, long context, and honest web access.
body: Reliability in AI leads the window alongside AI features and feedback loops, with a Bright Data talk detailing how web-accessing agents claim to have searched the live web while quietly falling back on stale training data behind blocks and CAPTCHAs [0]. Open-weights releases push agent capability further: MiniMax M3 targets coding agents with up to one million token context and Anthropic and OpenAI API compatibility [3], and GLM 5.2 is pitched as the new top open-source model across multimodal tasks [6]. Agent skills, scheduling, and fixed-point convergence break out on the technique side, while risk assessment surfaces in security framing as IBM weighs letting agents manage and reset compromised passwords [2]. Jensen Huang separately argues society needs new social norms for AI [7].

cited_items.title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
